commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e5029b7e9ded4acd43ade793c6ae1393f61fe631)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a shared `/stream`/`/streaming` chat command that stores `SessionEntry.streamingMode`, gates support with a channel capability, and wires session preview-streaming overrides into Telegram, Discord, and Slack with docs and tests.

PR surface: Source +205, Tests +182, Docs +11. Total +398 across 27 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection of PR head shows `/stream status` only reads `SessionEntry.streamingMode`, and the inline directive parser has no stream extraction. I did not execute tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor code/docs changes, real transport proof, and maintainer acceptance of the new session/capability contract before merge.

target.title: feat: add session stream mode command
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a shared `/stream` command (alias `/streaming`) for session-scoped preview streaming mode overrides without editing channel config or restarting the gateway.
- Persists the canonical mode on `SessionEntry.streamingMode`; accepted modes are `off`, `partial`, `block`, and `progress`. The command accepts `final` as a user-facing alias for canonical `off`.
- Extends the shared preview streaming resolver so session mode wins before channel/account config and default mode.
- Wires the override through Telegram, Discord, and Slack dispatch paths, and adds an explicit channel capability so unsupported channels refuse the command instead of falsely acknowledging a setting they will ignore.
- Intentionally out of scope: `/progress` / `streaming.preview.toolProgress` from #80989, Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix runtime wiring, and protocol/TUI patch support.

## Linked context

Closes #74077

Related #80989
Related #81009

Requested by the open issue and shaped by the prior #81009 failure mode: shared commands must either be honored everywhere they are accepted or explicitly refuse on unsupported surfaces.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `/stream` / `/streaming` can set a per-session preview streaming override for capable channels, and incapable channels no longer receive a false success acknowledgement.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux (`/root/projects/openclaw`), branch `clawsean/stream-mode-session-command`, based on current `origin/main` at branch creation.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/discord/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/stream-mode.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:prod`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): targeted Vitest completed 5 shards / 88 tests; `pnpm tsgo:prod` exited 0; `git diff --check` exited 0.
- Observed result after fix: tests cover command registration, set/status/reset, `final` -> `off`, unsupported-channel refusal/no persist, resolver session precedence, and Telegram/Discord/Slack helper precedence.
- What was not tested: live Telegram, Discord, or Slack UI behavior from a deployed gateway branch; Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix are intentionally unsupported by the new capability gate in this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this source checkout cannot safely restart/deploy the local gateway for live channel UI proof in this contribution pass. The PR therefore includes focused local runtime/unit coverage and explicit unsupported-channel behavior, with live UI proof left for maintainer/requested follow-up if required.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current `main` has no `/stream` command/session override; prior related PR #81009 demonstrated the failure mode where a shared command acknowledged an override only one channel honored.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/discord/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/stream-mode.test.ts` — passed, 5 shards / 88 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:prod` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `node scripts/check.mjs --timed` — blocked by stale `npm-shrinkwrap.json` guard before later gates; this PR does not change dependencies.
- Local `check-changed` child path passed core prod typecheck, then hit existing `packages/gateway-protocol/src/native-protocol-levels.guard.test.ts` type errors (`anyOf`/`oneOf` and implicit `any` parameters). New `/stream` test type errors found in that run were fixed, and a direct `pnpm tsgo:core:test` rerun then failed only on the gateway-protocol guard test errors.
- Testbox delegation was unavailable locally because `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: shared command exposure vs channel runtime honoring, the same class of issue that closed #81009.

Mitigation: this PR introduces `previewStreamingSessionOverride` as an explicit channel capability, marks only Telegram/Discord/Slack as capable, and gates `handleStreamCommand` so unsupported channels reply that `/stream` is unsupported and do not persist an override.

## Current review state

- Author implementation complete and pushed as one commit.
- Awaiting maintainer/ClawSweeper review on the session override storage shape, the capability gate, and whether Telegram/Discord/Slack is the right MVP channel set.
- Known caveat: live UI proof is not included yet; local runtime/unit coverage is included and explicit about limitations.
target.author: clawsean

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
